title: Why Venus Is an Inferno
script_summary: Exploring the transformation of Venus into a hellish environment due to a runaway greenhouse effect.

segments.voiceover: A runaway greenhouse turned Venus into a crushing, scorching world.
Imagine a planet where the temperature reaches 900 degrees Fahrenheit. That's Venus.
Once similar to Earth, Venus underwent a dramatic change, trapping heat like a furnace.
Its thick atmosphere, rich in carbon dioxide, creates extreme pressure—over 90 times that of Earth.
This runaway greenhouse effect not only scorched the surface but also created sulfuric acid clouds.
Curious about more planetary mysteries? Hit that follow button!
